auth_notes: Requires Client ID and Client Secret to obtain a Bearer token. Some legacy systems may use static API Keys or SOAP-based WS-Security.
gotchas: Complex nested JSON objects for loss details and claimant information
Strict state machine transitions (e.g., cannot move from 'Closed' to 'In Progress' without specific permissions)
Binary data handling for claim photos and PDF reports requires multi-part form data
client_must_provide: Scope permissions for claims:read and claims:write
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Developers will need to manually define the JSON body for claim creation as schemas are often industry-specific (ACORD standards). Webhook registration is typically handled via API (POST /webhooks) rather than a UI, requiring a one-time setup workflow.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Developers will need to manually define the JSON body for claim creation as schemas are often industry-specific (ACORD standards).
